{"key_event":"Xinhai Revolution and May Fourth mobilization","reasoning":"The fall of the Qing and post-1916 warlord fragmentation left the central state with weak fiscal, military, and administrative reach despite coercive regional armies. Civil society strengthened through parties, chambers of commerce, newspapers, students, New Culture activism, and the May Fourth movement, though it remained mostly urban and vulnerable to military repression.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":6.6,"state_change":-1.0,"state_power":3.2}
